Now, considering fault adjacency: this occurs when a nearby code change affects the same system as another bug. Since multiple tests that rely on correct offset handling started failing after this change, it's likely that the mutant introduced new issues adjacent to other parts of the system.

Moreover, fixing one test (TestMonthDay_Properties) might have inadvertently caused other areas relying on offset logic to fail because the mutation altered a fundamental behavior—returning UTC for any non-positive offset. This could have ripple effects across the codebase where time zones are used and expected to behave in specific ways based on their offsets.
